sutta: on a certain occasion the venerable ananda was staying among the koreans there is a place called sapooga in the township of the kolians then a great number of the sappoogians of the kollian clan came to visit the venerable ananda and on coming to him saluted him and sat down at 1 side as they said thus the venerable ananda said this to the kolians of sappooga men of the leopard's path these 4 factors of exertion for utter purification have been fully proclaimed by that exalted 1 who knows who sees that arahant the fully enlightened 1 for the purification of beings for the overcoming of grief and distress for the making an end of sorrow and lamentation for the winning of the method for the realization of nibbana what are the 4 the fact of exertion for the utter purification of moral conduct the utter purification of mind at the purification of view and the fact of exertion for the utter purity of release and what men of the leopard's path is the factor of exertion for the utter purification of moral conduct in this case among his virtuous he undertakes and practices the precepts this is called utter purification of morals he results i will bring to perfection such a purification of moral condom if it be incomplete and if complete i will supplement it here and there by wisdom the desire to do the effort exertion endeavor persistence mindfulness and attention applied there to is called a factor of exertion for the utter purification of morals and 1 men of the leopard's path is the factor of exertion for the utter purification of mind in this case a monk alou from sense desires aloo from unwholesome conditions enters upon the first jhana which is accompanied by thought directed and sustained born of seclusion delightful and pleasurable and abides therein then by the calming down of thought directed and sustained he enters upon the second jana the inward calming of the self that 1 pointedness of mind apart from thought directed and sustained that is born of concentration delightful and pleasurable and having attained it abides therein then by the fading out of the light equanimous mindful and composed he experiences in in his own body that happiness of which the aryans declare he is equanimous and mindful he who is equanimous and mindful dwells happily so he attains and abides in the third jana then by the abandoning both of pleasure and discomfort by the ending of both unh happiness and unhappiness felt before he attains the fourth jana a state of neither pleasure nor discomfort and utter purity of mindfulness and equanimity and having attained it abides therein this is called the utter purification of mind he resolves i will bring to perfection such a purification of mine if it be incomplete and if complete i will supplement it here and there by wisdom the desire to do the effort etc and attention applied thereto is called a factor of exertion for the purification of mind and what men of the leopard's path is the factor of exertion for the utter purification of you in this case among comes to understand as it really is this is dukkha this is the arising of dukkha this is the ending of dukkha this is the practice going to the ending of dukkha this is called utter purification of you he resolves i will bring to perfection such a purification of you it if it be inc complete etc etc the desire to do the effort and attention applied thereto is called a factor of exertion for the utter purification of you and 1 man of the leopard's path is the factor of exertion for the utter purification of release in this case the aryan disciple who is possessed of this factor of exertion for the utter purification of moral conduct of mind of you cleanses his heart of conditions that defile he releases his heart by means of conditions that release so doing he attains the perfection of release this is called the utter purification of release results i will bring to perfection such a purification of release if it be incomplete and if complete i will supplement here here and there by wisdom the desire the effort the exertion endeavour persistence mindfulness and attention applied there too is called a factor of exertion for the utter purification of release so men of the leopard spa these are the 4 factors of exertion for utter purification which were fully proclaimed by the exalted 1 who knows who sees the arahant the fully enlightened 1 for the purification of beings for the passing over of grief and distress for the making and end of sorrow and lamentation for the winning of the method for the realization of nibbana so in this suta the buddha is talking about the 4 purifications and the first 1 of moral kandang that means the person keeps the precepts the second 1 the utter purification of mind chitta visudi is the attainment of the 4 jaanas and the third 1 is the utter purification of view that means he understands the 4 noble truths and the 4 aryan truths and then the utter purification of release that means he attains the release the emancipation the enlightenment in this uttar the most important thing and i'd like to point out is the utter purification of mind chitta visudi because this suta makes it very clear what is chitta visudi purification of mine there is 1 suta in the majima nikaya called the rata vinita where it was explained that there are i think 7 stages 2 enlightenment and 1 of the stages is chitta visuji but in that suta it is not explained exactly what is the purification of mind and in the visuri maga the statement that the purification of mind is upachara samadhi threshold concentration and also apana samadhi which is the janus but that contradicts the suta here because in this is very clear that purification of mind is the 4 janas and the right concentration of perfect concentration in the sutas there's 2 ways of defining 1 is the 4 janus the other 1 is 1 pointedness of mind so this utah makes it very clear that at the purification of mine is the jana now because the dhyanas are mentioned uh the 4 janas are mentioned in detail i'd like to take this opportunity to go through the explanation of the 4 janas and that which is uh read just now and it was stated now that a monk alou from sen's desires aloud from unwholesome conditions enters upon the first jhana so you see to be able to enter into the first jhana you have to leave behind the sense desires that means the realm of the um the 6 senses you have to cut them offline the sense of not paying attention to them and aloof from unwholesome conditions that means to be able to enter the first jana you got to leave behind unwholesome conditions which means the jhana is a very wholesome condition because you leave behind unwholesome conditions then only you can enter into jannah so this is the first thing we have to be quite clear that chana is a very wholesome condition it is not an unwholesome condition as some people try to make it out now and then it is accompanied by thought directed and sustained uh it is in the first jana there is still some uh thought but then there is a suta in the majima nikaya see is that this kind of thought now is only skill thoughts you know it is not unskilled thoughts because unskilled thoughts have ceased uh in the first jhana so you are not just thinking of unwholesome things but only thinking of what is concerned with that state of ghana that putting your attention there being diligent and trying uh telling yourself probably that you have to remember the steps you took to attain to that state and then then it is born of seclusion that means seclusion normally in the sutras there are 2 types of seclusion vibeka 1 is body seclusion the other 1 is seclusion of the mind body seclusion that means to get away from people go to a quiet place seclusion of the mind means to to get rid of unwholesome defilements and wholesome conditions so it is born of seclusion and then it is delightful and pleasurable these 2 words are pitty and sukkah this pithi and sukha is a very subtle perception it is not a strong perception in the jaanas because the mind is so concentrated so strong that this pt and sukkah it is it it is not strong it is just a very subtle feeling and this is confirmed in the diga nikaya and uh and so uh a person abides in the first jhana then the second jhana by the calming down of thought directed and sustained that means when you enter the second jana you leave behind the thoughts there is no more thoughts in the second jana he enters upon the second jana the inward calming of the self that 1 pointedness of mind apart from thought directed and sustained that is born of concentration and this state it is a very concentrated state where all the thoughts have ceased and cannot arise the mind is so mindful uh 1 thing about these states of concentration they are not mindless states they are very mindful states and the the more concentrated your mind is and the more intense the constant the the mindfulness very intense concentration is like a point of light a focus and the mind is focused to 1 point it is delightful and pleasurable again then by the fading out of delight mindful and composed he experiences in his own sorry by the fading out of delight equanimous mindful and composed he experiences in his own body that happiness of which the aryans declare he who is equanimous and mindful dwells happily so he attains and abides in the third jana so you see in this third jana the mind is very tranquil economist and mindful this word mindful is is always is always there in the jaanas you can't say that when a person enters the jana he doesn't know it is very mindful he's very very mindful and then by the abandoning both of pleasure and discomfort by the ending of both happiness and unhappiness felt before he attains the fourth jhana a state of neither pleasure nor discomfort and utter purity of mindfulness and economy equanimity and having attained it abides therein so in this fourth jhana you leave behind the feelings pleasurable and unpleasurable feelings bodily as well as pleasurable and unpleasurable mental feelings leaves behind and then and then only you can enter the fourth jana a state of neither pleasure nor discomfort and utter purification of mindfulness and equanimity so in this fourth jana the mindfulness is utterly pure that's why because the mindfulness is utterly pure such a high degree of mindfulness that's why the buddha said that perfect mindfulness is the fourth jana so the chana states are not to be belittled you know they are states of very high attainment concentration now we come to another suta 4 point uh 20.1 9 5 on a certain occasion the also exalted 1 was staying among the sakyans at kapilavatu in banyan park at that time vapa the sakyan a disciple of the unclothed went to visit moghlana the great and on coming to him saluted the venerable mauglana the great and sat down at 1 side as he said thus the venerable moglana the great was saying this to vapa the sakyan disciple of the unclothed there may be someone here vapa restrained in body speech and mind owing to the waning of ignorance and the arising of knowledge now vapa do you see any cause owing to which the asavas causing pain would flow in upon the man in at some future time sir i do see such a reason there may be in this case a certain previously done evil deed whose fruit has not yet ripened owing to that the asava's causing pain might flow in upon that man at some future time at this juncture the conversation which the 2 were holding was broken off then they exalted 1 in the evening rising from his solitary meditation went towards the service hall and on reaching it sat down on a seat made ready having taken his seat the exalted 1 said this to the venable mowglian or the great so the buddha said pray mawlana on what subject are you talking as you sit here now and what was the talk just interrupted lord i was just saying to vapa the sakyan disciple of the unclothed there may be someone here restrained in body speech and mind owing to the waning of ignorance and the rising of knowledge now vapa do you see any cause owing to which the asavas causing pain would flow in upon the man at some future time at these words lord vapa was saying to me sir i do see such a reason there may be in this case a certain previously done evil deed whose fruit has not yet ripened owing to that the asava's causing pain might flow in upon that man at some future time this was the subject we were engaged upon then the talk was interrupted by the arrival of the exalted 1 then said the exalted 1 to vapa the sakyan disciple of the unclothed if you vapa would allow what you deem allowable and reject what you deem should be rejected and in this case you should not and in case you should not understand the meaning of my words if you would question me still further thus how is this what is the meaning of that we might have a talk here i will indeed allow the exalted 1 what i deem allowable and i will reject what i deem should be rejected and in case i do not understand the exalted 1's words i will question the exalted 1 still further thus what is this lord what is the meaning of that so be let us have a talk here i'll stop here for a while to just comment this mug lana asked this question there may be someone here restrained in body speech and mind going to the waning of ignorance and the arising of knowledge do you see any cause going to which the asava's causing pain would flow in upon that man at some future time this ah saw us this word ask of us i would translate as uncontrolled mental outflows the outflows mental outflows are that we have no control over for example emotions um worries uh painful distressing thoughts etc [Music] and uh these are the ah so here uh because of the waning of ignorance and the arising of knowledge someone is restrained in the karmas it does not create new karma and on top of not creating new karma he has attained knowledge so is it possible for the asava's painful assabasa to arise in the future and this man this uh disciple of the uncle author said that it is possible now this although is not mentioned here i presume that this naked ascetic is a disciple of the jain niganta because the genes they have a belief that they have to punish themselves continuously punish and inflict pain on themselves to wear away the karma of the previous lives whereas the buddha said that it is not necessary that what is necessary is to attain enlightenment attain knowledge so you see here the buddha is challenging this naked ascetic to a debate you know that's why he said if you vapa would allow what you deem allowable and reject what you deem should be rejected and then etc then we might have a talk here and so he's stating the conditions for a debate and this uh naked aesthetic accepted these conditions then the buddha said now what do you think vapa as to these ah savants which come about as a result of bodily activities in the case of 1 who abstains from bodily activities that cause vexation and distress it follows that those asavas causing pain do not exist in him he does no fresh deed as to his former deed or kama he wears it out by constant contact with it by a wearing out that is plain to see not just for a time 1 that asks for inspection that leads onward a wearing out that can be understood by the intelligent each for himself now vapa do you see any reason why asava is causing pain should flow in upon him at some future time no law that cannot be i'll just stop here for a moment here the buddha is saying those karmas that result in painful asavas if that person stops doing it is it possible that those uh new uh painful assaults would arise so this uh this naked ascetic has to say no no then the buddha continue now again vapa what thank you as to those of us that come about as the result of the activities of speech as a result of activities of mind in the case of 1 who abstains from activities of speech and mind that cause vexation and distress it follows that those asava's causing pain do not exist in him he does no fresh deed as to his former deed he wears it out by constant contact by a wearing out that can be understood by the intelligent each for himself now vapa do you see any reason why asava's causing pain should flow in upon him at some future time no lord that cannot be again what thank you vapa as to those are causing vexation and distress which result from ignorance owing to the waning of ignorance and the arising of knowledge it follows that those asavas of taxation and distress do not exist in him he does no fresh deed as to his former deed he wears it out by constant contact with it by a wearing out that is plain to see not just for a time 1 that asks for inspection that leads onward a wearing out that can be understood by the intelligent each for himself now vapa do you see any reason why asava's causing pain should flow in upon him at some future time no lord that cannot be so then by the monk whose mind is perfectly released 6 constant abiding states are attained he seeing an object with the eye is neither elated nor depressed but rest indifferent mindful and comprehending hearing a sound with the ear smelling ascend with the nose tasting a server with the tongue with body contacting tangibles with mind cognizing mental states he is neither elated nor depressed but dress equanimous mindful and aware when he feels a feeling limited by body he knows that he so feels when he feels a feeling limited by life he knows that he so feels he knows when body breaks up after life is used up all my experiences in this world will lose their loyal and grow cold suppose vapa that a shadow is cast by a stump then comes a man with eggs and basket and cuts down that stump by the root so doing he digs all around it having done so he pulls up the roots even the rootlets and root fibers he chops that stump into logs and having done so chops the log into chips the chips he dries in wind and sun then burns them with fire then makes an ash heap the ash heap he winnows in a strong wind or lets the ash be carried away by a swiftly flowing river verily vapa that shadow cast because of the stump is cut off at the root made like a palm tree's thumb made not to become again of a nature not to arise again in future time just in the same way by a monk whose heart is thus released 6 constant abiding places are 1 he seeing an object with the eye etc etc with mind cognizing mental states is neither elated nor depressed but abides equanimous mindful and aware when he feels a feeling limited by body limited by life he knows that he so feels he knows when body breaks up after life is used up all my experiences in this world will lose their loyal and grow coal at these words disciple of the unclothed said this to the exalted 1 lord just like a man desirous of wealth who tends his property but gets no increase there from but instead gets tall and troubled for his pains even so long and did i desirous of prophet do service unto the unclothed but i got no prophet thereby but instead got tall and troubled for my pains i myself lord from this day forth whatever faith i had in those fools the unclothed i winnow it away in a strong wind or i let it be carried away by a swiftly flowing river it is wonderful lord it is marvelous may the exalted 1 accept me as his follower from this time forth so long as life shall last as 1 who has gone to him for refuge that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so you see here the buddha is saying that abstention from unskillful karma through body speech and mind will cause no new painful result of the parka to ripen and then the painful vipaka the painful the result of karma due to all karma is worn out by constant contact with it tolerating this karma as they arise and then then that person by contemplation by practicing the holy path meditation etc becomes liberated and 1 whose mind is liberated by equanimous mindful and aware even when dying that's why when the buddha said now when he's dying he knows when body breaks up after life is used up all my experiences in this world will lose their leo and grow coal and you can imagine like for example when we wake up after a dream suppose we had a nightmare and then sunday we wake up then we realized it's only a dream all that was so important in that nightmare is no more important so in the same way when we are dying all the things in the world that were so important to us that were so attractive etc as we are going to die here we find is no more important absolutely not important anymore that's why only karma is karma is very important if we do wrong karma then we know we have to pay for it if we do a lot of good karma we know we are going to be very happy after this that's why we have to be very very careful with karma and we practice the spiritual path it's not only the meditation that's important a lot of people think meditation now is the most important thing and we forget about the basics what is the basic basic ac la sila is keeping the precepts it is also moral conduct and in the sutas the buddha defines the moral conducive basically as having purity of the 3 karmas through the body speech and mind uh body speech and mind so sometimes uh people only think of the 5 precepts you know they forget about this basic teaching that they are commas of bodies peace and mind we have to be very very careful don't talk about bad about somebody who doesn't deserve to be talked bad about and don't have evil intentions or evil thoughts against somebody who the buddha says cultivates the holy path etc so we have to be very very careful
